title: Garlicky Creamy Greek Lamb Stir-Fry
description: This dish is a creamy and garlicky hangover-cure Greek meal featuring lamb loin chops seasoned with a tadka to complement the flavors of herbs and garlic.

ingredients.name: heavy cream
ingredients.notes: full-fat for better texture and flavor

instructions: Season lamb chops with paprika and freshly ground black pepper.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat. Add butter; when melted, add minced garlic, onion, and thyme leaves. Sauté for about 2 minutes or until fragrant.
Add tomato paste to the pan and stir for another minute until well combined with the onions.

Slice lamb chops into thin pieces if they are not already in bite-size pieces. Add them to the pan and sear both sides, cooking for approximately 3-4 minutes each side, or to your desired doneness level.
Remove from heat and let rest while preparing the sauce. In a separate saucepan, whisk together heavy cream, softened butter (remove from the fridge), fresh basil leaves, and salt until smooth.
Pour sauce over the lamb chops in the pan, cover with a lid or aluminum foil to keep warm, and cook on low heat for about 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld together.
